retrospective.harder_than_needed: AC-MIGRATE-3's literal wording ('every non-muted record whose post-backfill expiresAt is at or before now') and Design Decision 3 / AC-MIGRATE-6 pull in opposite directions if read too literally: DD-3 requires that migrateInviteLinks, called again at every later app startup, must never re-stamp expiryNotified on a NORMAL (non-legacy, already-fully-created) link that expires sometime after creation — that notification is the not-yet-built S4 sweep's job, not migration's. AC-MIGRATE-3's text has no explicit carve-out for that case. Reconciling the two required treating AC-MIGRATE-6's precondition ('a store it already migrated') as the load-bearing constraint: a fully-migrated, non-muted record must be a *permanent* no-op regardless of how much later now advances, which only holds if the AC-MIGRATE-3 stamp is gated on expiresAt itself having been the field that was missing (a genuinely legacy record) rather than on 'non-muted and currently expired' in general. Landed on that gate and added a dedicated regression test ('a second call at a LATER now still changes nothing') to pin it, plus post-impl question VQ-S1-020 asking the examiner to re-derive the same reasoning independently.
